findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Remote installer is fetched from the network and piped directly to bash
findings.description: The skill instructs the agent/user to download a shell script from an external domain and execute it immediately with `bash`. This creates an arbitrary code execution and supply-chain risk, because the remote server controls what runs on the host. The same pattern is repeated in the CI example, expanding the blast radius to automated environments.
findings.remediation: Do not use `curl | bash` for installation. Distribute reviewed installer code inside the skill package or use a trusted package manager with pinned versions, checksums/signatures, and explicit user confirmation before execution.

findings.title: External download source is obfuscated with base64 decoding
findings.description: The installer URL is hidden behind a base64-decoding step before execution. Obfuscating the true source impedes review and can be used to evade simple detection or manual inspection of the remote endpoint being trusted.
findings.remediation: Use a plain-text URL, document its provenance, and add integrity verification so reviewers can clearly see and validate the remote source.
